ASBESTOS CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-14-2023 Set For Trial On Jan-02-24 At 11:15 A.M. In Dept 503

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Dec-28-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES Via CourtCall:
Shaghig D. Agopian of BUTY & CURLIANO (510-267-3000), appearing for defendant and moving party Sacramento Stucco Co.
Steven J. Patti of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff and opposing party Marjorie Hawkins et al.
Taylor Matsumoto of BERKES CRANE SANTANA & SPANGLER LLP (213-955-1150), appearing for defendant Red-White Valve Corp.

RULING:
Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Sacramento Stucco Co.'s Motion for Summary Judgment is DENIED. Opposition filed by Plaintiffs. Reply filed by Defendant. Defendant fails to sustain its initial burden of showing Plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain needed evidence of asbestos exposure and causation. (Aguilar v. Atl. Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826, 855.)

Plaintiffs identified the deposition transcript of Decedent Arthur Hawkins, Jr., taken in In re Petition of Candy Knight, Sacramento Superior Court Case No. 34-2018-00230424, as a defendant-specific document in this wrongful death case. (Ohlsen Dec., Ex. C [Plaintiffs' Amended Response to Defendant's Special Interrogatories, Set One] at 10:17-19.) Decedent testified that as to the brands of compound he got from Defendant, he knew Gold Bond was one and Kaiser was another. (See Ohlsen Dec., Ex. D [Decedent's Testimony from In re Petition of Candy Knight, Sacramento Superior Court, Case No. 34-2018-00230424] at 134:12-15.)

The Court cannot infer Plaintiffs have no case against Defendant simply because Decedent did not clarify what the compounds are, or because he failed to state the name of other brands for other products supplied by Defendant. (See Scheiding v. Dinwiddie Const. Co. (1999) 69 Cal.App.4th 64, 81 [stating a court "can infer nothing at all with respect to questions which were neither asked nor answered"].) Defendant fails to foreclose the possibility that Plaintiffs may yet prove asbestos exposure and causation attributed to Defendant at trial. Since Defendant does not sustain its initial burden, the Court need not rule on Defendant's objections to Plaintiffs' opposing evidence, which are immaterial to the motion's disposition. (See Code Civ. Proc., sec. 437c, subd. (q).) Order signed in open court. Judge: Samuel K. Feng, Clerk: A. Mok. Not reported. (503/SKF)

RULING:
Hearing held, contested, and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiffs' Motion for Order Granting Preference in Setting Case for Trial, and Extending Discovery Cutoff is GRANTED under C.C.P. section 36(a). Opposition filed by Sacramento Stucco Co. Joinder to Sacramento Stucco Co.'s opposition filed by Red-White Valve Corp. The Court did not consider the opposition filed by Frank M. Booth, Inc. for failure to lodge courtesy copies with the Court under Local Rule 2.7B. Plaintiff's evidence is sufficient, for purposes of this motion, to show that she has a substantial interest in the action as a whole and that her health is such that preference is necessary to prevent prejudicing her interest in the litigation. (Code Civ. Proc., sec. 36, subd. (a); Code Civ. Proc., sec. 36.5; Fox v. Superior Court (2018) 21 Cal.App.5th 529, 534.) The trial date is January 2, 2024 at 11:15 a.m. in Department 503. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. section 36(f) is January 12, 2024. Local Rule 20 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is December 28, 2023. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Fact discovery cut-off date is December 15, 2023. Expert discovery cut-off date is December 29, 2023. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than October 12, 2023. If plaintiffs submit documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents.
